disclaimer: Educational assessment. CARSI is an IICRC-approved CEC provider; this is CARSI-original Australian training, not IICRC certification.
note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: Air Quality and Odour: Identification and Deodorisation Essentials — Course Quiz

questions.questionText: According to the course, what does our sense of smell actually detect when we perceive an odour?
options: Volatile chemical compounds, called odorants, that become airborne and stimulate receptors in the nose
Fine dust particles that settle on the lining of the nose
Sudden changes in indoor air pressure
High humidity acting directly on the sinuses
questions.sourceRef: M1 — What is Odour and Why it Matters

questions.questionText: A musty, earthy smell in a building most commonly indicates which underlying problem?
options: High concentrations of VOCs off-gassing from fresh paint
Hidden moisture supporting mould and microbial growth, producing MVOCs
Combustion residues left over from a recent fire
Outdoor agricultural odours drawn in through the ventilation system
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Categories of Odours and Their Characteristics

questions.questionText: Which deodorising approach does the course describe as especially suited to biological odours such as pet urine and decomposing matter?
options: Ozone generators operated in occupied rooms
Masking agents used as the primary treatment
Enzyme-based (biologically active) cleaners
HEPA filtration used on its own
questions.sourceRef: M7 — Deodorisation Methods and When to Use Them

questions.questionText: How does ventilation primarily help to control odours?
options: By sealing the building so that odours cannot escape
By diluting contaminated air with fresh outdoor air and exhausting odorous gases
By raising the indoor temperature to mask the smells
By adding fragrance to the incoming air supply
questions.sourceRef: M6 — Ventilation and Air Movement Considerations

questions.questionText: What does the course identify as the essential first step in any deodorisation effort?
options: Applying a pleasant masking fragrance
Running an ozone generator for several hours
Addressing and removing the odour source
Installing plug-in air fresheners throughout the space
questions.sourceRef: M7 — Deodorisation Methods and When to Use Them

questions.questionText: What is the key safety difference between ozone generators and hydroxyl generators?
options: Ozone must be used only in unoccupied spaces, while hydroxyl generators can be used safely with occupants present
Both ozone and hydroxyl generators may be operated with occupants present
Hydroxyl generators must be used only in sealed, unoccupied rooms
Ozone is safe to use in occupied spaces and leaves no harmful byproducts
questions.sourceRef: M8 — Equipment Setups for Odour Control

questions.questionText: Which statement about air filtration devices for odour control is correct?
options: HEPA filters remove gaseous odours, while activated carbon filters capture particulates
HEPA filters capture airborne particulates but do not remove gaseous odours, whereas activated carbon adsorbs odour gases and VOCs
Both HEPA and activated carbon filters only remove particulates
Activated carbon filters never need to be replaced once installed
questions.sourceRef: M8 — Equipment Setups for Odour Control

questions.questionText: After treatment, what provides objective evidence that odour levels have actually been reduced?
options: Asking occupants to sign off without taking any measurements
Measuring only the temperature of the room
Relying on the strength of a masking fragrance
Using a photoionisation detector (PID) to measure VOC concentrations, ideally against a baseline taken before treatment
questions.sourceRef: M10 — Final Odour Clearance and Verification
